basics.name: Jordan Avery Reyes
basics.label: Backend Engineer
basics.summary: Backend engineer with seven years building reliable data and payments services. I care about clear interfaces, observable systems, and keeping on-call quiet.

work.summary: Owned the ledger and settlement services behind a multi-currency payouts product.
highlights: Cut settlement reconciliation time from hours to under five minutes by redesigning the batch pipeline.
Led the migration of 40+ services from a shared database to bounded per-service schemas with zero downtime.
Reduced p99 API latency by 38% through query profiling and targeted caching.

work.summary: Built ingestion and reporting features for a customer-facing analytics dashboard.
highlights: Designed an idempotent event-ingestion API handling 12k events per second at peak.
Introduced contract tests that cut integration regressions by roughly half.

projects.name: ledgerlint
projects.description: A static checker that flags unbalanced double-entry ledger transactions before they reach production.
highlights: Catches a class of accounting bugs at commit time instead of in nightly reconciliation.
